steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Rhetorical Synthesis question asking which choice best emphasizes the order in which the two films were released. The decisive clue is the goal word “emphasize” paired with “order” — the correct choice must make the release sequence the central point, not simply mention both films.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Alf’s Button (1930) was released early in Merle Oberon’s career, whereas Forever and a Day (1943) came out years later.’: Uses temporal markers (“early” and “years later”) to place both films in sequence and make release order the main idea. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘Early in her career, Merle Oberon wasn’t listed in some film credits, such as the credits for the film Alf’s Button, where she played a nameless, uncredited role.’: Discusses only one film and focuses on credit status, not release order. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘Merle Oberon was famous for her roles in films like Alf’s Button (1930) and Forever and a Day (1943).’: Mentions both films but frames them as examples of fame, not in any temporal sequence. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘In Alf’s Button (1930), actress Merle Oberon played a nameless, uncredited role; however, in Forever and a Day (1943), she played a credited role—that of Marjorie Ismay.’: Contrasts the type of role in each film (uncredited vs. credited), not their release order. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
